channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S. Economy Grew Modestly at End of 2025
item.description: Gross domestic product expanded at a 1.4 percent annual rate in the last quarter of the year, hit by the effects of the government shutdown.

item.title: GDP growth sharply slows to 1.4%, less than half of fourth quarter expectations
item.description: Economists say the small bump in unemployment rates is due to the Trump Administration's immigration crackdown stalling population growth.

item.title: This math graduate applied to over 1,000 jobs and still didn’t hear back—so he moved to Austria
item.description: Gen Z and millennial workers in Britain are so fed up with being ghosted by employers that some are giving up on the country altogether.

item.title: Head of Russia’s Antidoping Agency Was Involved in 2014 Doping Scheme, Whistle-Blower Claims
item.description: The accusation surfaced as Russia tries to re-establish its Olympics eligibility.

item.title: Why the United States Is Seeing an Ongoing Tourism Slump
item.description: Last year, the U.S. was the only major destination to see a decline in international travelers. With increased scrutiny at the border, ICE violence an...

item.title: Eric Dane, McSteamy on ‘Grey’s Anatomy,’ Dies at 53 After Battling ALS
item.description: His breakout role came in 2006 as the handsome Dr. Mark Sloan, nicknamed McSteamy, the head of plastic surgery at a Seattle hospital. He died 10 month...

item.title: First, Colbert, now ‘The View’: Trump’s FCC opens equal time investigation into political chat show
item.description: “The FCC has an enforcement action underway on that,” Chairman Brendan Carr told reporters after an agency meeting Wednesday.

item.title: How Modi’s AI handholding moment backfired as Sam and Dario refused to play along
item.description: “I didn't know what was happening,” Altman later said, adding he was “confused, like when (Modi) grabbed my hand and put it up."

item.title: Former Prince Andrew’s Arrest Upends Royal Family’s Effort to Move Past His Scandal
item.description: King Charles III’s family, long rocked by infighting and grievous losses, is facing what could be the gravest threat to its moral authority in more th...

item.title: Something big is happening in AI, but that’s the only thing Matt Shumer got right
item.description: This could be the greatest democratizing event of all time.

item.title: A Cancer Detection Test Fails in Major Study
item.description: A closely watched clinical trial in Britain that screened blood for early detection of cancer did not show a reduction in later stages of the disease.

item.title: Former Prince Andrew’s Arrest Upends Royal Family’s Effort to Move Past His Scandal
item.description: King Charles III’s family, long rocked by infighting and grievous losses, is facing what could be the gravest threat to its moral authority in more th...

item.title: As Trump Considers Second Iran Attack, It Could Be Deadlier Than the First
item.description: Within days of the U.S. strikes on Iran’s nuclear facilities last year, all sides agreed to a cease-fire. This time could be different.

item.title: In Ukraine, Senators See War’s Impact and Press for Stronger U.S. Support
item.description: Senate Democrats traveled to Kyiv and Odessa to show solidarity with the war-torn nation and make the case that the United States should do more, incl...

item.title: A New U.S. Blockade Is Strangling Cuba
item.description: An analysis of ship movements shows that the Trump administration is isolating the island at one of its most vulnerable moments.
